# macList — Radio playout & live assist, native on the Mac

> A radio playout / live-assist app for hobby radio makers on macOS, built as an
> homage to mAirList (the Windows original). Private hobby project in active
> development — honest early stage, no download offered yet. Openly vibecoded
> with Claude Code by Henning Halfpap (https://henning.halfpap.io/).

## What it is

A tool to drive your own radio show rather than just play it back. No server,
no subscription, no cloud: start it and run your hour.

Features today (Iteration 10):

- Two players side by side (live assist): one runs, you load and fire the next.
- Playlists as `.m3u` — the open format, nothing proprietary.
- Sectioned, sortable playlist; drag tracks in straight from Finder.
- Backtimer — countdown to the target time so handovers land on the second.
- Widget bar with studio clock.
- Played tracks stay visible (dimmed) — that is show history, not garbage.
- Bilingual: German and English.

## What it is NOT

- Not a commercial product: no App Store, no price, no support contract.
- Not a mAirList replacement or clone — pro consoles, multi-station, royalty
  reporting and regionalization are deliberately out of scope.
- Not finished: real playout depth (cue points, auto-cue, PFL, automation) is
  ahead, not behind. If you need something dependable for your station, use
  mAirList (https://www.mairlist.com/).

## Why it exists

mAirList was the last remaining reason to boot Windows. macList moves that last
tool to the Mac — a bow to the original, not an attack. Formula:
mAirList provides the blueprint. The Mac the hardware. Claude Code the skill.

## Tech

Swift 6.3 · SwiftUI · AVAudioEngine/CoreAudio · macOS 14+ · core managers
(`PlaylistManager`, `PlayerController`, `OutputRouter`, `SettingsStore`) all
`@Observable`/`@MainActor`. The repository is private; this page is the proof
of existence. Roadmap: https://maclist.halfpap.io/assets/roadmap.svg

## Contact

- Author: Henning Halfpap — https://henning.halfpap.io/
- Email: henning@halfpap.io
